response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished web and distributed-systems engineer whose career centres on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing components for a C IDE and a bachelor-thesis audio project—far below the 8–12 years of production C++ expected here. He has no industrial automation domain knowledge, no familiarity with IEC 62443 or Secure Development Lifecycle practices, and no experience with the industrial protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, etc.) listed as core or advantageous requirements. His genuine strengths in event-driven micro-services and Agile practices do not compensate for the fundamental technical mismatch.
strengths: Demonstrated high-throughput, event-driven systems design (millions of HTTP req/s at Dunnhumby using async I/O and low-latency patterns)
Strong Agile/TDD discipline with consistent application of pairing, trunk-based development, and CI/CD across multiple employers
Some C++ background exists (NI LabWindows/CVI components, wave-field-synthesis thesis) providing a non-zero foundation to build on
concerns: C++ is not a practiced primary language—only ~6 months of professional use 10+ years ago; the candidate's own skills list omits C++ entirely
No industrial automation, embedded, or IoT domain experience and no knowledge of required industrial protocols (MQTT at scale, OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850)
Zero evidence of security-focused engineering: no SDL practices, no IEC 62443 familiarity, and no mention of application-security responsibilities in any role
